D2 Vision L3 Apply Reference

Successive Contrast & Recovery

slide the adapting colour and watch a neutral patch carry its opponent tint, then recover over time

Adaptation biases the opponent channels. A neutral surface viewed afterwards is read against that bias, so it appears tinged with the complementary hue until the bias decays.

Slide the adapting colour. The patch begins steeped in the complementary tint, then fades to true neutral as the curve below tracks the recovering channel — successive contrast in time.
